title: AI Delivery Associate
key_evidence: Requirements are forgiving and operator-shaped: 'early-career delivery experience' in project coordination/implementation/customer-facing ops; organised/dependable; 'comfortable with ambiguity... work without a fully defined playbook'; 'technically curious — you don't need to be an engineer'; data-literate with dashboards/spreadsheets; clear communicator. Ryan's founder-associate (turned ambiguous priorities into workstreams, cross-functional execution) + community/ops history maps cleanly; the no-engineer + ambiguity framing removes blockers.

Company: Gradient Labs founded 2023 by ex-Monzo/Pleo/Google team; raised $13M Series A (Apr 2025, Redpoint) then doubled to $26M (Octopus/CommerzVentures); 'small but mighty team of 40+'; revenue grew 900%; agents serve 32M end users. London-based.
Posting published 2026-06-05 — only ~4 days old at run date. FRESH posting = minimal accumulated applicants = lower competition window; apply now.
SCREENING: ~40-person Series A team — first gate is very likely a hiring manager / AI Delivery Lead reading CVs, not a heavy ATS funnel. Good for Ryan's non-traditional but high-agency story.

DIFFERENTIATION caveat: role is implementation/delivery coordination (onboarding, configuring the agent from playbooks, monitoring perf, compliance/security-review support). Ryan is community-led growth, NOT classic customer delivery/implementation; he is adequate-to-good here, not spiked. Domain = financial services (regulated) — outside his crypto/AI home turf, and the role touches compliance/due-diligence/governance which favours a fintech-ops background.
Conversion: associate band fits Ryan's mid-level; London role, in-office cadence not stated, salary not stated (Series A London likely clears or near £50k).
biggest_lever: Very fresh posting (~4 days) at a small ~40-person founder-led Series A team with forgiving, ambiguity-friendly, no-engineer-required requirements that match Ryan's founder-associate/cross-functional-execution profile — a human hiring-manager read in a low-competition window.
biggest_risk: Core of the role is customer delivery/implementation + compliance/governance in regulated financial services — adjacent to but not the same as Ryan's community-led growth, so he meets the bar without standing out, and a fintech-ops candidate would be more directly differentiated.
apply_verdict: Prioritise (apply now while fresh) — strong requirements/screening fit and a brand-new posting at a small founder-led team; the main caveat is that delivery/implementation isn't Ryan's spike, so frame his ambiguity-navigation and cross-functional founder-associate execution hard.
